description: Templates offered in the New Site dialog (Sites.vue advanced mode). Each entry's `fields` object is spread into the newSite reactive object when the user picks a template. Templates deliberately do NOT hardcode a phpVersion — the wizard's auto-picked active PHP (via loadPhpVersions) wins, so pressing a template doesn't wipe a version that works for this user's installed binaries. SSL is on by default for PHP templates because login cookies / CSRF tokens should always be HTTPS. Keep IDs stable — they're referenced by saved drafts and user documentation.

templates.label: PHP (generic)
templates.notes: Vanilla PHP site — document root points at the folder with your index.php / front controller.

templates.label: WordPress
templates.notes: Classic LAMP stack. SSL is on so wp-login cookies + admin POSTs are protected.

templates.label: Laravel
templates.notes: Document root should point at the app's /public directory.

templates.label: Symfony
templates.notes: Document root should point at the /public directory.

templates.label: Nette
templates.notes: Document root should point at the /www directory.

templates.label: Next.js (Node proxy)
templates.notes: Node upstream port must be configured after site creation.

templates.label: Node.js (generic proxy)
templates.notes: Any Node/Bun/Deno app — set the upstream port after creation.

templates.label: Static HTML
templates.notes: No runtime. Apache serves files from the docroot directly.
